title: Emmy winner Amy Poehler spotted at popular Somerville coffee shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Amy Poehler was spotted at Diesel Cafe in Somerville, where she took a photo with an employee.
Poehler is a Primetime Emmy and two-time Golden Globe winner, known for 'Parks and Recreation' and 'Saturday Night Live'.
The cafe jokingly suggested its visit from Poehler elevated its status, sharing the encounter on social media.

title: Oscar-winning actress Emma Stone visits North End restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Emma Stone visited Arya Trattoria on Hanover Street in Boston's North End on Thursday.
Arya Trattoria, known for its Italian cuisine, posted an Instagram photo of Stone's visit.
Stone joins other celebrities like Dwayne Johnson and Joe Mazzulla who have visited the restaurant.

title: Adam Schefter explains how ‘Hard Knocks’ finally landed the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots will appear on 'Hard Knocks' in 2027, following the Seattle Seahawks in 2026.
The Patriots agreed to participate after the NFL pressured teams, citing changed selection rules and removal of playoff exemptions.
ESPN's Adam Schefter explained that the Patriots committed by agreeing to the NFL's 'next year' participation strategy.

title: Mass. high school pitcher Brody Bumila’s fastball tops 100 mph, draws crowd of MLB scouts
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brody Bumila, a high school pitcher from Bishop Feehan, hit 100 mph on the radar gun against Hopkinton.
Nearly 50 MLB scouts attended Bumila's game, where he struck out nine batters in three innings.
Bumila is committed to the University of Texas but may enter MLB Draft, with Red Sox as potential contenders.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (April 3, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Hawksmoor, a renowned British steakhouse, will open its third U.S. location in Boston's Seaport in Fall 2026.
Jazz Urbane Cafe, a jazz club and restaurant, will open in May 2026 in Nubian Square, focusing on Southern cuisine.
Fast-casual chain NAYA expands with a new location in Framingham, set to open in late summer 2026.

title: Daughter runs Boston Marathon to honor dad with rare vision disorder
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Virginia runner London Tuma to run 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass Eye and Ear research.
Tuma honors her father, Jon, who has Usher syndrome affecting his vision and hearing.
Running in memory of their bond, Tuma aims to raise awareness and support for Usher's research.

title: BU rally pushes back on Pride flag removals, calls for policy change
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University students, faculty, and staff protested the removal of Pride flags outside the Silber Administrative Center on Thursday.
Petition with over 2,000 signatures delivered to BU officials, urging alteration of the signage policy to allow public-facing displays.
University cites Events and Demonstrations Policy for removals; protestors argue action contradicts BU's values of inclusion and free speech.

title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston designer Megan Lam created an MBTA-themed sweater featuring commuter rail and various subway line motifs.
Lam's hand-knit sweater was inspired by her experiences commuting in Boston and her job at Harvard Magazine.
The sweater's debut on Instagram garnered significant attention, highlighting Lam's unique approach to fiber arts.

title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 18-year-old Emmie Laudano from Wellesley will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
Laudano has trained for the marathon through the Dreamfar program, running Heartbreak Hill weekly.
She aims to raise funds for the Tierney Learning Center, supporting children's educational needs.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge on recent Greater Boston stories.
Peter Chianca has been the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.
The quiz covers trending news in Greater Boston to keep residents informed.
